title: Elephantopus plurisetus
taxonomy: Elephantopus plurisetus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Elephantopus plurisetus is classified within the genus Elephantopus, which consists of several species of plants, primarily found in tropical and subtropical areas. The plant is known for its unique characteristics and growth habits, which set it apart from other species in the same genus. Elephantopus plurisetus is further classified into different subspecies based on its geographical distribution and morphological variations.

intro: Elephantopus plurisetus, commonly known as Elephantopus, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Elephantopus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
